title: rubus spoliatus
intro: Rubus spoliatus, also known as the thimbleberry, is a deciduous shrub native to North America, characterized by its edible, dark red fruit and white or pink flowers. The medicinal properties of Rubus spoliatus include its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of consuming Rubus spoliatus include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and providing antioxidant properties. Rubus spoliatus can be prepared as herbal teas, tinctures, and jams, with its fruit and leaves often used in traditional medicine and culinary practices.
